name: Ronnie Scott
abstract: Ronnie Scott (born Ronald Schatt; 28 January 1927 – 23 December 1996) was a British jazz tenor saxophonist and jazz club owner. He co-founded Ronnie Scott's Jazz Club in London's Soho district in 1959.

concerts.label: Stéphane Grappelli and the Diz Disley Trio / Ronnie Scott and His Trio
Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Delfeayo Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: We were playing Ronnie Scott’s. ... So we had a gig that brought me into London on Saturday, which was their last – Elvin’s last night at the club
about_quotes.doc_title: Delfeayo Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Josephine Davies
about_quotes.evidence: trying to get a gig at places like Ron Scott’s where I love playing Ronnie Scott’s and it’s very high profile and it’s a great venue, and incredibly hard to get a gig there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Josephine Davies interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 14, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: I can't do that within my office, except to call Mary Scott who was the wife of Ronnie Scott. She lives two blocks from me, and she, she arranges those gigs.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: DeFranco Buddy
about_quotes.evidence: it was Ronnie Scott who said, “why don’t you guys play together, play one or two numbers at the end of the set together?” And we did
about_quotes.doc_title: DeFranco Buddy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pat LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: We played Ronnie Scott’s every year. The same as Buddy’s band. Buddy would play Ronnie Scott’s. Elvin would play Ronnie Scott’s.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: During my stay in England, I met many English musicians... Ronnie Scott, owner of the famous club known under his name in London
